findings.title: URMA failover triggers on healthy windows instead of unhealthy ones
findings.impact: IsUnhealthy returns true when successCount is greater than total * threshold, so a healthy window above the configured success-rate ratio can trigger a worker switch, while a fully failed or low-success window does not. With the default 0.5 ratio, 10/10 failures will never request failover, and 10/10 successes are classified unhealthy, breaking the core client-side URMA data-plane failover behavior.
findings.fix: Invert the predicate to match the documented strict-low threshold, e.g. return static_cast<double>(snapshot.successCount) < static_cast<double>(total) * successRateRatio; and keep the equality case healthy.
